title: Earthy, Quenched American Mushroom Mushimono
description: A comforting, aerated, and earthy plant-based-friendly dish featuring gochujang and mushimono-steam.

ingredients.name: shiitake mushrooms
ingredients.notes: fresh or dried, sliced 1/4 inch thick

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and sauté for an additional minute.
Add the sliced shiitake mushrooms and sauté for 5 minutes, or until they release their liquid and start to brown.
In a small bowl, whisk together the gochujang, rice vinegar, and soy sauce. Pour the mixture over the mushrooms and stir to coat.
Add the chopped green onions and sesame seeds to the saucepan and stir to combine.
In a small bowl, whisk together the cornstarch and water until smooth. Pour the mixture over the saucepan and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened.
Transfer the mixture to a serving dish and garnish with additional green onions and sesame seeds, if desired.
Steam the mushrooms for 2 minutes using a mushimono steamer or a steamer basket with a plate on top of the saucepan.
Remove the mushrooms from the steamer and serve hot, garnished with additional green onions and sesame seeds, if desired.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional green onions and sesame seeds, if desired.
